# Test Quality Review: lib/utils/ + test/utils/

**Scope:** `lib/utils/` (23 source files) · `test/utils/` (28 test files, incl. 5 helper/non-test files)
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/location_fallback_test.dart` — 672 lines for `DeviceLocation` tests. Its setup weighs ~100 lines per test case. Extract a `_TestBench` class (see AGENTS.md) to hold `mockJournalDb`, `mockLoggingService`, and `deviceLocation` and reduce per-test boilerplate. A natural split would be: one file for non-Linux path (`getCurrentGeoLocation` with permission variations) and one for the Linux portal path (`getCurrentGeoLocationLinux`). **RESOLVED (partially):** the boilerplate is extracted — `buildDeviceLocation({ipProvider, linuxBackendFactory})` replaces all 14 inline constructions, `stubRecordLocationFlag({enabled})` replaces all 15 flag stubs, and the setUp moved from inline getIt boilerplate to `setUpTestGetIt(additionalSetup: ...)`. The two-file split is rejected: AGENTS.md forbids splitting one source file's tests across multiple files.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/screenshots_platform_test.dart` — 434 lines. Five logical test groups (Linux portal, macOS screencapture, Linux tools, file-path helpers, window-management) could each become their own focused file once the source is clarified. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the proposed per-group split would put five test files on one source file, which the one-test-file-per-source rule forbids; the five groups already organise the file internally.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/screenshots_comprehensive_test.dart` (385 lines) + `test/utils/screenshots_flatpak_test.dart` (394 lines) + `test/utils/screenshots_test.dart` (250 lines) + `test/utils/screenshots_portal_integration_test.dart` (256 lines): four files for one 187-line source (`lib/utils/screenshots.dart`) plus service code that lives in `lib/services/portals/`. These should be consolidated into one `screenshots_test.dart` (covering `lib/utils/screenshots.dart`) and the portal/Flatpak coverage should move to `test/services/portals/`. **RESOLVED:** done — comprehensive + portal-integration were already consolidated; the remaining `screenshots_flatpak_test.dart` was deleted outright: its 'coverage' stubbed mocks and asserted on the stubs (never touching production code), while the real portal coverage lives in `test/services/portals/screenshot_portal_service_test.dart`. One mirror remains for `lib/utils/screenshots.dart`.
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/screenshot_consts_test.dart` — 262 lines testing 92-line constants file. Each constant is asserted in at least two places (individually and in consistency checks). Consolidating redundant cross-checks would halve the file.
  **RESOLVED:** removed the `Configuration Validation` group entirely — its `all tools have configurations` test was a verbatim duplicate of `Screenshot Tool Configurations → should have configuration for all Linux tools`, and `all tool configurations should have valid argument lists` was a zero-value type-existence smoke check. Also dropped the now-redundant `should have configuration for all Linux tools` test, folding its per-tool presence check into the richer `Constants Consistency → Linux tools list should match tool configurations` loop (which already iterates every tool). The literal-value pins (`Tool Arguments` group) and the wiring checks (`Constants Consistency → tool arguments should match configuration arguments`) are complementary — one pins the constant values, the other verifies the configs reference those constants — so both are kept.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/location_fallback_test.dart:48-50` — `MockLocation` and `MockLocationData` are defined inline. They should be moved to (or already exist in) `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. Inline definitions bypass the centralized stubs and can diverge from CI expectations.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/screenshots_test.dart:17-19`, `screenshots_platform_test.dart:19-21`, `screenshots_comprehensive_test.dart:22`, `screenshots_portal_integration_test.dart:15` — `MockWindowManager` is defined independently in four test files. One definition in `test/mocks/mocks.dart` would serve all four.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/task_utils_test.dart:27-35` — `TaskStatus.inProgress(...)` is tested twice with identical assertions (lines 27–34 and 35–41). One call is an exact copy-paste. The second adds zero information; remove it. **RESOLVED:** the verbatim duplicate `TaskStatus.inProgress` assertion is deleted.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/location_test.dart` — tests two source files (`lib/utils/geohash.dart` and `lib/utils/location.dart`) in one file. Per AGENTS.md, one test file per source file. `getGeoHash` tests should move to `test/utils/geohash_test.dart`. **RESOLVED:** done — `geohash_test.dart` already subsumed all four cases (worked example, origin, extremes via the any-coordinate Glados property); the group was removed and `location_test.dart` now mirrors only `lib/utils/location.dart`.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/modals_test.dart` — tests `lib/widgets/modal/modal_utils.dart`, not any `lib/utils/` file. Should move to `test/widgets/modal/modal_utils_test.dart` (or wherever the widget test lives). Both `modalTypeBuilder` tests assert the same `WoltDialogType` outcome, making the small/large distinction untested. **RESOLVED:** done — deleted; the canonical `test/widgets/modal/modal_utils_test.dart` covers small/large/exact-breakpoint with genuinely distinct runtimeType asserts.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/task_utils_test.dart` — tests `lib/classes/task.dart`, but `test/classes/task_test.dart` already exists. These tests should be merged into the canonical file. **RESOLVED:** done — the colorForBrightness coverage moved into `test/classes/task_test.dart` as a parameterized per-status loop; `task_utils_test.dart` deleted.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/screenshot_consts_test.dart:141-175` — The six per-tool `should have complete configuration for X` tests each assert only `config.arguments == Xconstant`. This duplicates the `Constants Consistency` group at the bottom of the same file. The per-tool tests add no new information once the consistency loop exists. **RESOLVED:** done — the four per-tool copies were removed; the Constants Consistency group carries the per-tool argument equality.
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/segmented_button_test.dart:37` — `expect(find.text(testLabel), findsOneWidget)` alone proves nothing beyond "widget built". The test should also verify that the `semanticsLabel` defaults to `label` when unspecified, and overrides it when a custom value is supplied.
  **RESOLVED:** (already addressed in the test file by a prior batch — confirmed true, no change) the file now carries `uses label as semanticsLabel when semanticsLabel is not provided` (asserts `Text.semanticsLabel == label`), `uses explicit semanticsLabel when provided` (asserts the override + value pass-through), and an exhaustive 5×4 label × semanticsLabel matrix asserting `semanticsLabel ?? label` and `value` for every combination. The original `findsOneWidget` test also retains a value-field assertion, so no vacuous test remains.
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/immediate_exit_test.dart:19-23` — `expect(immediateExit, isA<Function>())` tests existence, not behavior. Per AGENTS.md "no constructor smoke tests" rule, this has zero value. The two FFI-resolution tests are legitimate; the function-type check should be removed.
  **RESOLVED:** deleted the `immediateExit is a callable function` test. The two FFI-resolution tests remain and still cover `canResolveImmediateExit` (which transitively forces the same `_posixExit` symbol lookup `immediateExit` would use). The `lotti/utils/immediate_exit.dart` import stays valid because `canResolveImmediateExit` is exported from it.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/location_test.dart` — `getGeoHash` has four example tests; three assert only `isNotEmpty`. A Glados property would: (a) verify output length is always 12 chars (default GeoHasher precision), (b) verify latitude ∈ [−90, 90] and longitude ∈ [−180, 180] never throw, and (c) verify that distinct coordinate pairs produce distinct geohashes with high probability (collision test). This is a genuine pure-function gap. Generator shape: `combine2(floatInRange(-90,90), floatInRange(-180,180))`.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/file_utils_test.dart` — `relativeEntityPath` is currently tested by one concrete example per entity type (9 hand-coded cases). A Glados property parameterising over `JournalEntity` types with generated IDs and dates would verify: (a) paths always end with `.json`, (b) the folder segment matches `folderForJournalEntity`, (c) the suffix matches `typeSuffix`, (d) the date subfolder matches `createdAt`. This would replace ~120 lines of copy-pasted `entityPath` assertions with one property.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/file_utils_test.dart` — `resolveJsonCandidateFile`: only two concrete cases tested (one normal, one traversal). A Glados property should verify: (a) any non-traversal relative path resolves inside `docDir`, (b) any `../`-prefix path throws `FileSystemException`. **RESOLVED:** done — two Glados properties: generated non-traversal folder/tail combinations (with and without leading slash) always resolve within docDir via `p.isWithin`, and any `../`-prefixed path at depths 1–6 throws.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/segmented_button_test.dart` — `buttonSegment` is pure (transforms label/value/semanticsLabel into a `ButtonSegment`). A small Glados property parameterised over `(String label, String? semanticsLabel)` could verify the `semanticsLabel ?? label` default and that the returned `value` field always equals the input. **RESOLVED:** (adapted) Glados bodies cannot host the BuildContext the helper requires, and the input space is tiny — an exhaustive 5×4 label × semanticsLabel matrix inside one testWidgets now covers the full contract deterministically.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/timezone_test.dart` — `getLocalTimezone` trims whitespace from the Linux file path. A Glados property over `(String timezone, String leadingWs, String trailingWs)` would prove the trim is correct for any whitespace variant, not just the two example strings tested. Generator: `combine3(choose([...timezones]), whitespace, whitespace)`. **RESOLVED:** (stale) `timezone_test.dart` already carries exactly this property ('trims any leading/trailing whitespace from the timezone file').
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/fd_limits_test.dart` — `resolveFdSoftLimitPlan` has seven discrete example tests covering all logical branches. Glados would add value for the clamping arithmetic: a property over `(softBefore, hardBefore, target)` triples where `softBefore < target && target > hardBefore` should always yield `newSoft == hardBefore`. The discrete coverage is already strong, so this is low priority.
  **RESOLVED:** added four Glados properties (with `tags: 'glados'`) plus four private record generators (`_FdPlanCase` with `toString()`, reshaping raw `intInRange` seeds into the exact ordering each branch needs so no body-level filtering is required): (1) the requested clamp case — `soft < target && target > positive hard` always yields `newSoft == hardBefore` and never exceeds the cap; (2) target-fits case — `soft < target <= hard` raises exactly to `target`; (3) satisfied case — `soft >= target` is left untouched (`newSoft == softBefore`); (4) unlimited-soft case — a negative (RLIM_INFINITY) soft reading stays satisfied and is never lowered to target. numRuns are 120/120/120/96 (all in 80..180, ≠100).
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/image_utils_test.dart` — `getRelativeAssetPath` (null case): the generator `_GeneratedAssetPathScenario` hard-codes `includePlatformMarker: false` as "null" when the marker is absent. A separate `null`-input generator would verify the `absolutePath?.split(marker).last` null-safety guard for free.
  **RESOLVED:** added a Glados property (`numRuns: 96`, `tags: 'glados'`) over `choose([true, false])` asserting `getRelativeAssetPath(null, isAndroid: x) == null` for both platform branches, proving the `absolutePath?.split(...)` null-safety guard holds on each path. The two existing concrete null tests are kept as worked examples.

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/utils/geohash.dart` — no dedicated test file. The single exported function `getGeoHash` has 3 of 4 test cases with vacuous assertions (`isNotEmpty` + `isA<String>()`). Concrete behavior (12-char output, monotone latitude encoding for constant longitude, distinct outputs for distinct inputs) is untested. A `test/utils/geohash_test.dart` file should be created, taking over the tests currently embedded in `location_test.dart`.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/utils/screenshots_*` (five test files) — `lib/utils/screenshots.dart` is not fully covered by any single file; coverage is scattered across files that also test `lib/services/portals/`. The actual function `takeScreenshot` integration path through `screenshots.dart` is not verified independently of the portal service.
- [x] **[MED]** `lib/utils/consts.dart` — no test file. All symbols are `const` strings/bools; no behavior. The AGENTS.md one-file-per-source rule requires a corresponding test file even for trivial files. A minimal test that imports the file and asserts a representative subset prevents silent renames breaking consumers. **RESOLVED:** done — `consts_test.dart` pins the representative persisted flag strings plus the scroll-alignment default.
- [x] **[MED]** `lib/utils/platform.dart` — no test file. `isTestEnv` (`Platform.environment.containsKey('FLUTTER_TEST')`) has observable behavior: under `flutter test` it should be `true`. A test asserting `isTestEnv == true` when running under test would both document and protect this. **RESOLVED:** done — `platform_test.dart` asserts `isTestEnv` under the runner plus the desktop/mobile mutual exclusion.
- [x] **[MED]** `lib/utils/cache_extension.dart` — the three exported `Duration` constants (`dashboardCacheDuration`, `entryCacheDuration`, `inferenceStateCacheDuration`) have no assertions anywhere in `cache_extension_test.dart`. A test verifying their values prevents accidental silent changes. **RESOLVED:** (stale) all three duration constants are pinned in `cache_extension_test.dart` with change-warning reasons.
- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/utils/audio_utils.dart` — `getFullAudioPath` is tested with a single concrete example that exercises the happy path. The null/empty directory edge case is untested (though the function signature does not accept nulls, so this is minimal risk).
  **RESOLVED:** the function cannot receive null (`audioDirectory`/`audioFile` are non-nullable `String`), so the reachable boundary is the empty-string case. Added a test that builds a `JournalAudio` with `audioDirectory: ''` and `audioFile: ''` and asserts `getRelativeAudioPath` is empty and `getFullAudioPath` degrades to exactly `mockDocDir.path` (no spurious separator, no throw).
- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/utils/segmented_button.dart` — `semanticsLabel` override path is completely untested. The only test passes no `semanticsLabel` and does not inspect `semanticsLabel` at all.
  **RESOLVED:** (already addressed in `segmented_button_test.dart` — confirmed true, no further change) the override path is covered by `uses explicit semanticsLabel when provided` (asserts the supplied value lands on `Text.semanticsLabel`) and by the 5×4 matrix which exercises non-null `semanticsLabel` values across every label. Same coverage that resolves the corresponding `[LOW]` test-quality item above.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/file_utils_test.dart` — contains four Glados tests at `numRuns: 160` and one at `numRuns: 120`, for a budget of 760 runs. The `normalizeAttachmentIndexKey` property (160 runs) and the `isAgentPayloadPath` classification property (120 runs) operate on simple string segments; 80 runs each would still surface regressions. Reducing these two from 160+120 to 80+80 saves 120 runs (~15% of the file's Glados budget) with no meaningful loss of coverage. **RESOLVED:** done — 80 + 80.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/image_utils_test.dart` — three Glados tests at `numRuns: 160` each (480 total) all share the same `_GeneratedImagePathToken` enum with only 6 variants. With 6 values per dimension, the combinatorial space is small; 80–100 runs per property is sufficient. Reducing to 100 each saves 180 runs. **RESOLVED:** done — all three at 100.
- [x] **[MED]** `test/utils/string_utils_test.dart` — two Glados tests at `numRuns: 160` (320 total). The idempotence test uses `any.stringOf(...)` with a small character alphabet (15 chars); 100 runs is adequate. The scenario-model test has a richer structure and benefits from 120–160. Net saving: ~60 runs if the idempotence test is reduced to 100. **RESOLVED:** done — idempotence at 100, the scenario-model property stays at 160.
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/audio_utils_test.dart` and `test/utils/image_utils_test.dart` — the audio-path and image-path generators produce structurally identical shapes (directory prefix parts + stem + extension + documents parts). Both sets run at 160 runs despite the token space being only 6 values. Neither has failed with fewer runs historically; 100 each would be safe.
  **RESOLVED:** `image_utils_test.dart` was already at 100 for its three properties (prior batch). Lowered the two `audio_utils_test.dart` path properties from `numRuns: 160` to `96` (kept ≠100 to respect the config-value rule); the new null-asset and any-platform properties also use 96. The 6-value token space makes the extra runs redundant.
- [x] **[LOW]** `test/utils/screenshot_consts_test.dart` — 262 lines of tests for a 92-line constants file. No Glados, so no execution-speed concern per se, but the redundant consistency assertions (each constant asserted twice — once inline, once in the `Constants Consistency` group) double the test count without adding coverage.
  **RESOLVED:** same change as the file-size `[LOW]` item above — the duplicate `Configuration Validation` group and the redundant per-tool config-presence test were removed, so each tool/argument relationship is now asserted once in `Constants Consistency`. The remaining double-coverage (literal value pins vs. config-wiring checks) is intentional and complementary, not redundant: one would catch a changed constant value, the other a mis-wired config map.

**Total numRuns budget for test/utils Glados suite:** 3,280 runs across 21 Glados tests. Applying the reductions above (file_utils: −120, image_utils: −180, string_utils: −60, audio: −120) would bring this to ≈ 2,800 runs, a 15% speed improvement with no meaningful regression risk.

---

## Summary

- **Oversized impl files (≥ 1000 lines):** 0. All 23 source files are well within limits (largest: `fd_limits.dart` at 307 lines).
- **Oversized test files (≥ 500 lines):** 1 (`test/utils/location_fallback_test.dart`, 672 lines). Four screenshots test files together total 1,719 lines for one 187-line source file.
- **One-file-per-source rule violations:** 8 test files are either orphaned (no `lib/utils/` counterpart), misplaced (`modals_test.dart` → `lib/widgets/`, `task_utils_test.dart` → `lib/classes/`), or split (`location_test.dart` covers two sources; five `screenshots_*.dart` files cover one source). **This is the largest structural problem in the scope.**
- **Weak test files:** 4 files with meaningful quality issues: `location_test.dart` (vacuous geohash assertions), `segmented_button_test.dart` (incomplete behavior coverage), `immediate_exit_test.dart` (smoke-only function-type check), `task_utils_test.dart` (duplicate `inProgress` assertion + misplacement).
- **Inline mock violations:** 2 files define mocks outside `test/mocks/mocks.dart`: `location_fallback_test.dart` (`MockLocation`, `MockLocationData`) and four `screenshots_*.dart` files (each defining `MockWindowManager` independently).
- **Glados candidates (genuine pure-function gaps):** 4 high/medium opportunities: `getGeoHash` (no property test at all), `relativeEntityPath` (9 copy-paste examples ripe for one property), `resolveJsonCandidateFile` (traversal boundary), `getLocalTimezone` whitespace-trim.
- **Biggest opportunity:** Consolidate the five `screenshots_*_test.dart` files into one canonical `screenshots_test.dart` for `lib/utils/screenshots.dart` and move portal/Flatpak coverage to `test/services/portals/`. This alone removes ~4 inline `MockWindowManager` definitions, 1,500+ lines of scattered overlap, and brings the folder into compliance with the one-file-per-source rule.
